[00:00] Jamie Bridges (Rep): Thanks for making time again. How are things on your end since we last spoke?
[00:11] Curtis Brown (VP of Support, Globex): Busy, but good. Happy to dig in.
[01:20] Curtis Brown: The core issue hasn't changed - reporting is a nightmare - I'm exporting to spreadsheets every single week. It's the thing keeping me up at night.
[03:05] Curtis Brown: If we could deflect a third of tickets to self-service, my CFO's model puts that at about 2 million annually in headcount we wouldn't have to add. That's the number I'd be measured on.
[05:33] Curtis Brown: We are also evaluating Zendesk, just so you know the landscape.
[07:02] Jamie Bridges (Rep): Internal note - Curtis Brown is clearly our champion here, actively selling this internally on our behalf.
[09:30] Jamie Bridges (Rep): Great, I'll follow up with next steps over email. Appreciate the time.

To: Julia Brewer (VP of Support, Northwind Systems)

Hi Julia,

Best,
Cameron Ellison

Thanks, Julia

---

From: Warren Aguirre
To: Mrs. Nicole Powell MD

Hi Mrs.,

On sign-off: ultimately this needs Timothy Gross, our CFO, to approve the spend - she holds the budget for anything over 100k, so I'll need to bring her in.
Process-wise: my team runs the eval, then it goes to a security review, then finance for final approval.
Heads up that procurement requires a full legal review and our MSA redlines usually take about three weeks, so we should start that early.
And yes on requirements - ease of admin and total cost of ownership are the deciders.

Thanks, Warren
